species: antibodies against MAP1S phospho‐Ser900 and CEP131 phospho‐Ser35 confirmed CDKL5‐dependent phosphorylation of these targets in human cells.
cell_type: CRISPR‐/Cas9‐mediated genome editing was used to disrupt the CDKL5 gene in U2OS osteosarcoma cells modified with the Flp‐In™ T‐REx™ system.
disease_state: Mutations in the gene encoding the protein kinase CDKL5 cause a debilitating neurodevelopmental disease termed CDKL5 disorder.
sample_source: antibodies against MAP1S phospho‐Ser900 and CEP131 phospho‐Ser35 confirmed CDKL5‐dependent phosphorylation of these targets in human cells.
cell_line: CRISPR‐/Cas9‐mediated genome editing was used to disrupt the CDKL5 gene in U2OS osteosarcoma cells modified with the Flp‐In™ T‐REx™ system.
